elements.sent-2411-003: With the advent of current and future high-resolution CMB experiments, the kinematic Sunyaev-Zel’dovich (kSZ) effect has become a unique observational probe of the distribution of baryons and velocities in the Universe.
elements.sent-2411-004: In this work, we propose a novel binned bispectrum of the form temperature-temperature-density to extract the late-time kSZ effect from cleaned CMB maps.
elements.sent-2411-005: Unlike `kSZ tomography' methods, this estimator can use any tracer of the large-scale structure density field projected along the line-of-sight and does not require individual redshifts.
elements.sent-2411-006: With our method, we forecast signal-to-noise ratios (SNR) of $\sim$100-200 for the upcoming Simons Observatory (SO) and CMB-S4 correlated with a galaxy sample from WISE that is restricted to the linear regime.
elements.sent-2411-007: We also extend galaxy modes into the non-linear regime and explore this harmonic space to show that the SNR peaks for squeezed triangles that have a short (linear) density mode and long temperature modes in harmonic space.
elements.sent-2411-008: The existing kSZ$^{2 -$density projected-fields estimator compresses the rich information contained in this bispectrum across various scales and triangle shapes.}
elements.sent-2411-009: Moreover, we find that the lensing correction to our estimator's signal is relatively small.
elements.sent-2411-010: We study the dependence of this kSZ signal on $\Lambda$CDM parameters for SO and CMB-S4 and forecast initial constraints on the sum of neutrino masses while restricting to the linear galaxy bias regime.
elements.sent-2411-011: Our work illustrates the potential of the projected-fields kSZ bispectrum as a novel probe of baryonic abundance and beyond-$\Lambda$CDM cosmology with upcoming precision measurements.
elements.sent-2411-012: Introduction In recent years, high-resolution observations of the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB) by ground-based telescopes such as the Atacama Cosmology Telescope (ACT) \cite{ACT2016 and the South Pole Telescope (SPT) \cite{SPT2011 have made secondary anisotropies detectable at high significance.}}
elements.sent-2411-013: These observations open a window into the late-time Universe, going beyond the primary anisotropies that originated in the early Universe, which were measured by all-sky satellites such as Planck \cite{Planck2018 The kinematic Sunyaev-Zel’dovich (kSZ) effect \cite{SZ1972, SZ1980, Ostriker1986 arises due to Compton-scattering between CMB photons and free electrons with a non-zero bulk velocity; the resulting Doppler boosting of photons leads to a secondary CMB anisotropy that is proportional to the electron momentum along the line-of-sight (LOS).}}
elements.sent-2411-014: Thus, the kSZ effect has emerged as a probe of both astrophysics (e.g.\,\cite{F16, Battaglia2017, BattagliaHill2019, Amodeo2020, Bolliet2022 ) and cosmology (e.g.\,\cite{Bhattacharya2008, Mueller2014, P23, Mueller2014DE, Munch ), through its dependence on baryon density and peculiar velocity respectively.}}
elements.sent-2411-015: Since the kSZ effect maintains the blackbody spectrum of the primary CMB, it cannot be isolated from observed CMB maps using component separation techniques alone.
elements.sent-2411-016: The kSZ signal sourced by electrons in the late-time Universe is then detected by combining CMB data with Large-Scale Structure (LSS) observations through various statistical methods or `estimators'.
elements.sent-2411-017: The pairwise momenta of galaxies (e.g.\,\cite{Ferreira1999 ) was applied to ACT data along with BOSS spectroscopic galaxies leading to the first detection of the kSZ effect in 2012 \cite{Hand2012 , followed by others in Planck, SPT, and ACT data \cite{Planckpairwise, SPTpairwise, Calafut2021 with this approach.}}}
elements.sent-2411-018: The velocity-weighted stacking method \cite{Ho2009, Shao has been used to constrain the baryon density profile in ACT data with BOSS galaxies \cite{Schaan2021 and with DESI photometric LRGs \cite{Hadzhiyska2024 .}}}
elements.sent-2411-019: On the other hand, velocity reconstruction (e.g.\,\cite{Deutsch2018, Smith2018 ) has been forecasted to probe primordial non-Gaussianity \cite{Munch, Kumar2022 and the Universe on the largest possible scales \cite{giri2020, Cayuso2023 ; the first detection with this method was done recently in ACT data with DESI LRGs \cite{FMcCarthy2024 .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-020: All of these `kSZ tomography' estimators were shown to be mathematically equivalent - different ways of representing the $\langle Tgg \rangle$ statistic - a bispectrum\footnote{A bispectrum refers to the harmonic space equivalent of a three-point correlation function.}
elements.sent-2411-021: This is analogous to a power spectrum in harmonic space being the Fourier transform of a two-point correlation function.
elements.sent-2411-022: On the other hand, the novel kSZ estimator proposed in this work is a distinct bispectrum of the form $\langle TTg \rangle$.
elements.sent-2411-023: An existing method, the so-called `kSZ$^{2}\times\delta_g$' or projected-fields power spectrum involves filtering and squaring a CMB map in real space before cross-correlating with LSS \cite{Dore2004, D05 ; thus, it sums the $\langle TTg \rangle$ statistic across various triangle shapes and is a compressed version of our novel bispectrum.}
elements.sent-2411-024: The novel kSZ bispectrum here and the `kSZ$^{2}\times\delta_g$' estimator use galaxy fields that are projected along the LOS ($\delta_g$), so they only need a rough redshift distribution of the galaxies.
elements.sent-2411-025: Thus, a distinct advantage is that they are applicable to large-volume galaxy samples with large photometric redshift uncertainties, such as the WISE \cite{WISE and unWISE \cite{unWISE2019 catalogs; in fact, their frameworks can also be applied to any LSS tracer of the underlying density in place of the galaxy field, such as galaxy/CMB lensing convergence \cite{Bolliet2022 , 21-cm fluctuations to probe reionization (see \cite{LaPlante_2020_21kSZkSZ_BiS ), etc. In contrast, the `kSZ tomography' methods require individual galaxy redshifts to make significant detections (see e.g.\,\cite{K_Schmidt2013, Flender2016, BlochJohnson2024, FMcCarthy2024 , and are mostly limited to spectroscopic galaxies or photometric samples with small photo-z errors (e.g.\,DESI LRGs with $\sigma_z/(1+z)\lesssim0.02$ \cite{DESILRG_2023 ).}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-026: So far, there have been two kSZ$^{2}$$\times\delta_g$ measurements at $\sim$3-5$\sigma$ in Planck maps using WISE \cite{Hill2016, F16 and unWISE \cite{Kusiak2021 data.}}
elements.sent-2411-027: The proposed projected-fields kSZ bispectrum avoids a convolution over the CMB filter that otherwise occurs with the kSZ$^{2}\times\delta_g$ estimator, and instead of compressing information across triangle shapes, we bin the $\langle TTg \rangle$ statistic in 3D-harmonic space.
elements.sent-2411-028: Therefore, this novel estimator contains richer information that is better separated across different scales.
elements.sent-2411-029: This also allows us to restrict $\delta_g$ modes to the linear regime to avoid Halo Occupation Distribution (HOD) and non-linear galaxy bias uncertainties while maintaining high-fidelity; we forecast high signal-to-noise ratios of $\sim$100-200 with this novel estimator for SO$\times$WISE and CMB-S4$\times$WISE, which are comparable or higher than those forecasted with the kSZ$^{2}\times\delta_g$ estimator with unrestricted $\delta_g$ \cite{Bolliet2022, P23 .}
elements.sent-2411-030: We assume the best-fit $\Lambda$CDM model from Planck-2018 \cite{Planck2018 as our fiducial model.}
elements.sent-2411-031: This paper is organized as follows: In Section II we describe the theoretical formalism of our proposed kSZ bispectrum and provide details about its binning, the lensing contribution to this signal, and its covariance matrix.
elements.sent-2411-032: We summarize survey specifications, analysis choices, and numerical implementation in Section III.
elements.sent-2411-033: We present forecasts with this estimator for SO$\times$WISE and CMB-S4$\times$WISE in Section IV and discuss its information content across triangle shapes.
elements.sent-2411-034: In Section V, we study the cosmological dependence of this novel kSZ bispectrum on $\Lambda$CDM parameters.
elements.sent-2411-035: We also illustrate its potential beyond measuring the late-time baryon abundance by forecasting constraints on the sum of neutrino masses with this probe.
elements.sent-2411-036: We discuss these results and the future outlook with this novel estimator to conclude in Section VI.
elements.sent-2411-037: We begin with an overview of the kSZ effect and the existing kSZ$^{2}\times\delta_g$ estimator that has been used to detect it using projected-fields of matter overdensity.
